entry.title: Re: FM to update USR21
entry.content:
Hai all, 

Is there any report program to change the idtype 04 to 00 or 01 ins usr21 table without the address number, please provide the solution, if any.

Thank you

entry.title: How to Download Mail Attachments in CPI
entry.content:
I want to Download mail attachments In CPI 





SAP Build Process Automation  SAP Commerce Cloud SAP Integrated Business Planning for sales and operations

entry.title: Integrating Custom References with Sales Billing by Extending the Integration Flow
entry.content:
Introduction

You might already know that you can store references to related data, such as external documents, in SAP Subscription Billing by defining custom references. But did you know that you can transfer those custom references to Sales Billing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and display them on customer invoices? In this blog we’ll walk you through the steps to set this up. 

Example Use Case

You want to enter purchase order references in subscriptions and include those references in the items of billing document requests so the references can be displayed on invoices generated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

Before we dive into the setup, here’s a short summary of the concepts: 

Custom References and Custom Fields

  • Billing data in SAP Subscription Billing includes custom references that originate in subscriptions or in customer or product data.  
  • Custom references from SAP Subscription Billing can be mapped to custom fields in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, as we’ll describe in this blog post.  

To find out more, browse the following documentation on the SAP Help Portal: 
Custom References | Custom Fields

Custom Integration Flows in SAP Cloud Integration

SAP provides standard integration flows that define aspects such as mapping and routing for data integrations. As a customer or partner, you can extend these integration flows, for example to adapt the way data is replicated or to include additional data in replication. If you want to dig into the details of custom integration flows, refer to Integration Flow – Concepts on the SAP Help Portal. 

For our example, we’ll show how to extend the integration flow used to transfer bills from SAP Subscription Billing to SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

  

Configuration Steps

  1. Configure custom fields in SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
  2. Define and use custom references in SAP Subscription Billing.
  3. Extend the standard integration flow in SAP Cloud Integration.
  4. Check that the integration is working correctly.

1. Configure custom fields in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Create a custom field

To create a customer field, you need a role with all the following business catalogs:

  • SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_BLE
  • SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_FLD
  • SAP_CORE_BC_EXT_FLEX

Open the Custom Fields app and create a custom field Purchase Order. The business context must be Sales: Billing Document Item (SD_BILLINGDOCITEM).

 

 Adapt the user interface to show the new custom field
  1. In the Manage Billing Document Requests app, display a billing document request. Choose the billing document request number to navigate to the Billing Document Requests app. Choose the user option to adapt the UI:

  2. Search for the field Purchase Order and select it:


  3. The field Purchase Order appears on the billing document request item:

 

2. Define and use custom references in SAP Subscription Billing

  1. Open the Manage Business Configuration app, go to Custom References and define a custom reference for the purchase order. Enable the field for subscriptions and bill items: 
  2. Open the Manage Subscriptions app, open a subscription, and navigate to Custom References. Find the custom reference type Purchase Order and enter a purchase order ID/number:

  3. When a bill is generated for the subscription, the bill item contains the custom reference for the purchase order:

 

3. Extend the standard integration flow in SAP Cloud Integration

To modify the message mapping of the standard integration flow to include the purchase order field, the following steps are required in SAP Cloud Integration: 

  1. Download the standard message mapping 
  2. Check the post-exit processing in the standard integration flow
  3. Create a custom integration flow
  4. Enable the extension in the standard integration flow 
Download the standard message mapping

Start by downloading the standard message mapping from the standard integration flow Transfer Bills to SAP S4HANA Cloud. You need the message mapping to add it your custom integration flow later, where you can modify the mapping.

Check the post-exit processing in the standard integration flow

In the post-exit processing step (Post Processing), display the details of the Bundle Message step: 

 This step creates a new payload that contains the following: 

  • The original bill and customer payload 
  • The message mapping output

This payload will be passed from the standard integration flow to your custom integration flow. To find out more about post-exits, see Customer Exits in the SAP Cloud Integration documentation. 

Create a custom integration flow
  1. Create a custom integration flow with the ProcessDirect adapter and configure an address: 

    Next we’ll customize the post-exit integration flow.

  2. Enter the following namespace mapping: 
    xmlns:ns1=http://sap.com/xi/XI/SplitAndMerge

  3. Add a filter to get the original bill response, which is the source of the message mapping: 

  4. Upload the message mapping from the standard integration flow:

  5. Download the latest WSDL file from the communication arrangement SAP_COM_0095 and upload it to the custom integration flow:





  6. Modify the message mapping in the custom integration flow for the purchase order field. Map the custom reference ID to it:

     

  7. Deploy the custom integration flow. 
Enable the extension in the standard integration flow
  1. Configure the standard integration flow:

     ℹ️ The address must be the same as the address configured in the custom integration flow.

  2. Deploy the standard integration flow. 

4. Check that the integration is working correctly 

After the next transfer of billing data to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you see a reference to the subscription billing document request in the Manage Billing Data app:

When you click on the document ID, you jump into the billing document request item, where you can see that the purchase order is displayed:

🏁You've now completed the setup and can include custom references in your customer invoices. 

More Information 

  • Subscription Management with Sales Billing 
  • Adapting SAP Fiori UIs at Runtime 
  • SAP Cloud Integration - Integration Flow Extension 
  • SAP Subscription Billing 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales Billing

entry.title: BADI/User Exist CO40
entry.content:

I am looking BADI/User Exist which will allow me pass description from planned order to production order. (transaction CO40). 

Any ideas ?

entry.title: Re: CVI Conversion - Precheck master data errors
entry.content: You may check the CVI cookbook for info on overlapping number ranges.

entry.title: Change the idtype from 04 to 00 or 01 without the address number
entry.content:
Hai all,

Scenario, User is not having any address number in usr21 and with idtype 04. Is there any report program to change the idtype to 00 or 04 without the address number. I tried to maintain the address number, but it is not possible for idtype 04. If any advice or solution is helpful. 

Thank you in advance

entry.title: Re: What's your favorite tip for using SAP Build Apps?
entry.content:
Maybe my favorite tip. When entering the code to load your app in the SAP Build Apps preview app, the code is case IN-sensitive. Or should I saw NOT case sensitive?

What's more, you do not have to click Confirm pin, but instead can press Enter.

What does that mean? You can type in the code with one hand 😸

entry.title: Action doesn't change value in entity in ABAP RESTFull application
entry.content:
Hello,
I created RAP application on S4HANA on prem system. I added action which creates DB records for further using by other business object. App created these records based on user choice, after action finish its work I want to return something for current entity and I decided create field Criticality. Everything works  fine field Criticality is filled with proper value, but nothing changes in frontend, rows are not marked by app, value of field Criticality is present in structure RESULT of action. Marks are appeared once app saves criticality value into DB, but I don't want to save it into DB, I just want mark rows after action finish its work. App is managed without draft Odata version 2.

entry.title: Re: Deliver Real-World Results with SAP Business AI: Q4 2023 & Q1 2024 Release Highlights
entry.content: Amazed by what's been delivered for SAP Business AI so far! More to come at SAP Sapphire in June 🙂

entry.title: Receiver XI adapter in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi All,

Am trying a scenario, HTTP -->CPI-->S4(XI adapter). whenever am a sending a message from Postman, i could see two messages generated for one request from Postman and i can see only one message post to S4 System. In the screenshot i have entered correlation id, so it is displayed both the messages.

What could be the reason why it is generating two messages in CPI. Please share your suggestion.

Regards,

Mani

entry.title: Abapgit install only module import sap basis 700
entry.content:
Hello, i have a question about AbapGit.
in my company we work with a multiple customers. We create products to implement in clients, transporting requests using AL11 and CG3z may cause problems, but there is abap git and your implementation is only sap basis 702, there are some way to implement the import module from sap git in sap basis 700?

The principal problemn to implement abapgit is 
https://github.com/abaplint/abaplint/issues/147

entry.title: /PLMU/WDC_DIR_DO_OBJ_LINK Webdynpro has Sort and Filter option but it doesn't work.
entry.content: /PLMU/WDC_DIR_DO_OBJ_LINK Webdynpro has Sort and Filter option but it doesn't work. Adding the given on action SORT and FILTER option on table is not working as well.

entry.title: Re: Accessing Production Data in QA Environment
entry.content:
Yes, but you'll have to modify your report to such an extent that it won't be a true proof of concept (replacin many SQL select/fetch with RFC_READ_TABLE, etc.).
Is your quality assurance system so old that it justifies this effort?

entry.title: Description Change for Special GL Indicator S4Hana Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello

I would like to change the description for special GL indicators in S4Hana public cloud. Standard SAP provided "P - Payment Request" i would like to change the description.

Is it possible to do the above changes in S4Hana public cloud. If yes, kindly arrange the procedure.

entry.title: Re: Receiver XI adapter in SAP CPI
entry.content: I think it has to do with how the JMS is used here, but not entirely sure.

entry.title: Re: Page variable binded to boolean doesn't work
entry.content:
It's not clear to me what you mean:

  • "two containers and bind them to a boolean state" - do you mean you bind the Visible property to the boolean variable?
  • "one false and the other true" - you have 2 variables? Or you use a formula with a NOT operation?
  • "two containers" - one inside the other, parallel? one after each other?

Is the scenario you have 2 containers you want to bind its Visible property to a variable and then click to show one, then the other?

entry.title: Finding xml value based on attribute value in groovy script
entry.content:
Hi,

I have this payload

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<ReferenceInformation>
<AssignmentId>
<IdValue name="customid">JOB182741</IdValue>
<IdValue name="reference">1234XYZ</IdValue>
</AssignmentId>
</ReferenceInformation>
 
I want to find the IdValue where attribute name = "customid" and store that value in an exchange property
I created a groovyscript based on code snippets i found when searching but sofar i did not manage to make it work.
 
This is my groovyscript:
import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import java.util.HashMap;
def Message processData(Message message) {
       def xml = message.getBody(String.class)      
       def completeXml = new XmlSlurper().parseText(xml)
       def netive_jobid = completeXml.ReferenceInformation.AssignmentId.IdValue[@name="customid"].text()
       message.setProperty("NTV_JOBID", netive_jobid)
       return message
}
But i get 2 errors
1)   unexpected token: = @ line 7, column 86.
2)   TypeError: Cannot convert undefined or null to object
 
I am not sure where i am going wrong. 
Any help is appreciated
 
Kr
Robert

entry.title: Re: IW23 - Quality notification - DB Table for "Subject" field
entry.content:
This is a long text so use FM such as READ_TEXT to access data from STXH/STXL

Hint: Try to double-click the text area or the text change/display icon to navigate to fullscreen editor, there navigate in menu goto header to display the values to pass to read_text FM.  ((Basically should be like notification number, LTXT, QMEL and language)

entry.title: Re: Accessing Production Data in QA Environment
entry.content: I know it's very troublesome, but is there another way?

entry.title: Re: Custom Analytical Queries : create button disabled (S...
entry.content:
you need to go in application "Extensibility Settings" and activate Key User Extensibility

(in the Starter system this option is OFF by default).

entry.title: Re: Repeated Android build error
entry.content:
Thanks for the feedback on how you resolved the issue. I tried to clone the app, just like you were successful, but I still get the same error.
Can you tell me if the clone you made was through backup and replace or if you recreated it page by page?

entry.title: Re: My catalog does not appear in fiori launchpad
entry.content:
Hi  @MaximoSanchez 

Firstly, i am not able to understand the German at some places 
 
Did you get connect with Security in your team ?

As per my observation you missed this thing 
Semantic Object, 
T-Code: /n/ui2/semobj 

As i created a blog on this check out the steps of sequence:
https://suggu-sandeep.medium.com/sap-fiori-tile-creation-2023-2dc76cb952a8

If same issue let's come back & comment over here again...

Thanks, 
Suggu Sandeep.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - build service not working since 24 hours
entry.content:
@liardoWe are having the same problem with Runtime version 4.9.201, can you check what happened?
We are trying to build in AAB for Android, the error presented is the following:

Runtime version: 4.9.201; AB version: 2.1.14COMMAND:
NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=16000 npxrnv export -p android -s standalone_bundle -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.9.201 -r
FAILED with ERROR:
Command failed with exit code 1: npx rnv export -p android -s standalone_bundle -c standalone --ci --yes --skipRnvCheck --packageManager yarn --template @sapappgyver/orchestra-template-standalone@4.9.201 -r

Any information would be of great help, thank you.

entry.title: Release strategy in STO
entry.content:
Hi

I am creating PO for stock transfer order and it triggers release strategy, the release strategy should not there while creating stock transfer PO .

There is no release strategy that contains the STO doc type in CL24N, also the list of values for doc type characteristic doesn't show the STO

Any idea why the release strategy is triggered

entry.title: Re: Get your development through a 3SL landscape with Multi-Off
entry.content:
Hi,

if you would like to provide a complete installable component in BTP ABAP Environment, then you would have to create an Add-On. If this is not an option and you use abapGit, then the namespace has to be installed on the target system before any import. These are the options that are currently available.

Kind regards
karin

entry.title: Re: How to check mandatory fields in RAP
entry.content:
This is a nice summary and food for thought article.

I believe -as others have commented above- that this should be part of the standard coding, as soon as possible. Having to manually add a check for something that is mandatory (and already recognized on the UI as such) but could be implemented generically should be a top priority for RAP framework development.

As for the:

- non standard message -> define a class based exception inheriting from cx_static_check

- message using the name of the component -> map those to data elements, by using RTTS on the structure of the CDS entity, then map the data elements to text by reading their texts from DD04T

- implementation -> yes, it is a validation

entry.title: Re: Configuration and Security Analysis in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Best wishes, Nilabh, nicely articulated.

entry.title: Fill data in multiple reports by executing 1 function in process
entry.content:
Dear experts 

Currently, I have a process in PAPM consisting of four reports, each linked to a specific view function. Currently, I need to execute four separate functions (1 for each view function) within my process activities before I have results in all 4 reports. I'm interested in consolidating these four functions into a single activity execution. So I want to execute one step in the process, but I would still need to see data in the 4 different reports. Is there a function or workaround available to achieve this?

Best,

Nathan

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports bug suppressing a box (suppress formula)
entry.content:
Hi Don

I found the attach region, but there's no way to upload a report or zip file - even by renaming it.
"The file type (.rpt) is not supported. Valid file types are: jpg, gif, png, xml, txt, jpeg, jpe."

Can I send the report by mail - normally you have my address?

Patrick

entry.title: Re: Is there an API for maintaining Decisions in SAP Buil...
entry.content: Hi @MustafaBensan, I don't see any reason why SAP wouldn't support it. This API isn't deprecated at all nor it is stated on the documentation that it belongs to a sunset solution. But if you feel the need to have an official statement from SAP, you ought to get it from SAP Support via a ticket.

entry.title: SAP Managed Tags Links Don't Work on SAP Community
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I've just realized today that SAP Managed Tags Links Don't Work on SAP Community.  Whatever I click any tag it seems to be showing entire SAP Community posts. 

Example for SAP Datasphere: https://community.sap.com/t5/c-khhcw49343/SAP+Datasphere/pd-p/73555000100800002141

Example for MM (Materials Management) https://community.sap.com/t5/c-khhcw49343/MM+%252528Materials+Management%252529/pd-p/477297786799213261950044802925335

It's something to be fixed! I'm not be able to navigate through specific SAP Managed Tags.

Someone from SAP Community Support could you please check?

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Re: Why SAP killed Appgyver and made everything worse?
entry.content:
Hello EsmeeX,

As English is not my main language i didn't understand this part:

"...Please note that while we welcome any feedback about the community experience, this forum in particular is the Builders Group, so the topics here should be focused on SAP Build products specifically..."

Are you referring to the main subject of my post? Isn't SAP Build Apps the Community Edition? 

Although i didn't mentioned the tool specifically, the Appgyver Forum was part o Appgyver itself and we used to post about the tool and the forum in the same space.

You guys call this space SAP Community, but new SAP Build Apps was called Appgyver Community Edition so there is this first confusion, after there is SAP S4HANA, SAP STP Innovation, SAP Build CodeJam, SAP Build Proccess Automation, SAP This, SAP That, SAP Everything, its a exhausting overflow of information, labels and small things that we need to understand in a short time and even with "Joining from the AppGyver Forums? Start here in the Builders Group" the post is not clear about those differences, that are vital to understand the whole SAP ecosystem, and what we will face with this migration.

This is the transparency i was talking about, there is none! Please make a live to the new users or a short video explaining the changes and put in the https://forums.appgyver.com/ page and a link to redirect to the SAP Community, I know the team are facing a hard challenge with both migration at the same time, but if you guys are having a hard time imagine how us the users are?

I apologize for the choice of words and the harsh tone I used but in my case and many others, ours jobs, companies and clients rely on SAP solution, but some users don't argue about or fight for what we built, they just choose to migrate to another solution, because its easier, imagine that: its easier to configure tons of API endpoints, colors pallets, texts, automations and everything to build an app than argue and give a feedback for you guys, if that doesn't concern the team and the company i really don't understand nothing of anything.

I'll give feedback and fight for the tool as long as I can see hope of someone in the team to hear the community but frankly in this new community i don't see much...

entry.title: Error while trying to migrate interface object from SAP PO to Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While trying to migrate interface object from SAP PO to Cloud Integration using migration tool, I get the following error in SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration). I tried choosing different templates and the issue is not resolved and Could you please help us to resolve the issue, Thank you!

Regards

Ramu

entry.title: Re: How do i get a SAP SuccessFactors Sandbox environment?
entry.content: Sign up for Learning Hub and take advantage of the Live Access option.

entry.title: Warum ihr den SAP BTP Partner Summit 2024 nicht verpassen solltet
entry.content:
Der SAP BTP Partner Summit wird das größte SAP BTP Partner Event in der DACH-Region werden. Er wird vom 7. Mai bis 8. Mai 2024 im brandneuen Heidelberg Congress Center stattfinden. Diese Veranstaltung ist eine hervorragende Gelegenheit, sich mit mehr als 500 Teilnehmern aus der SAP BTP Partner Community zu vernetzen, zu lernen und auszutauschen. Ihr erfahrt alles über die neuesten Trends und Innovationen rund um SAP BTP, künstliche Intelligenz und dem Clean Core Konzept. Alles Themen, die die digitale Transformation von Unternehmen maßgeblich vorantreiben. Ihr bekommt Einblicke in die SAP BTP Strategie, Roadmap und Geschäftsmodelle, die Euch helfen, euer Geschäft zu beschleunigen und Eure Kunden zu begeistern.

Der SAP BTP Partner Summit bietet Euch ein spannendes und vielfältiges Programm, das auf Eure Bedürfnisse und Interessen zugeschnitten ist. Ihr könnt aus verschiedenen Formaten wählen, die Euch fachliche Schulungen, Expertisen und Inspirationen liefern. Ob ihr Euch für den Vertrieb, die Co-Innovation oder den Service interessiert, Ihr findet immer die passenden Sessions für Euch.

Wir starten am 1. Tag direct mit einigen Highlights, die Ihr nicht verpassen solltet:

  • Die Keynotes von hochrangigen SAP Führungskräften und externen Experten, die Euch wertvolle Einblicke in die SAP- und Partnerstrategie, Trends und zukünftige Entwicklungen geben.
  • Die Guided Tours, die Euch durch das  Heidelberg Congress Center führen und Euch die Möglichkeit geben, Partner und SAP Experten zu einem bestimmten Thema zu treffen.
  • Die Breakout Sessions, die in kleinerem Rahmen und interaktiver stattfinden. Ihr könnt aus einer Vielzahl von Themen wählen, die sowohl für den Vertrieb als auch für die Co-Innovation und den Service relevant sind.
  • Die Meet the Expert-Stände, die Euch eine einzigartige Gelegenheit bieten, direkten Kontakt mit SAP-Experten zu knüpfen und Fragen zu spezifischen Themen zu stellen. Ihr könnt Euch zu Technologie, Lösungen oder bestimmten Partner Programmen beraten lassen und Euer Wissen erweitern.
  • Das Networking mit SAP-Experten und Partnern, das unerlässlich für den Erfolg ist. Ihr habt viel Raum zum Netzwerken und könnt wertvolle Kontakte knüpfen, Best Practices austauschen und potenzielle Kooperationsmöglichkeiten erkunden.

Ein rauschendes Abendevent bietet die Gelegenheit die Ereignisse des Tages Revue passieren zu lassen und in entspannter Atmosphäre mit anderen zu diskutieren.

An Tag 2 steht das Thema Künstliche Intelligenz im Vordergrund der Veranstaltung. Produktstrategies, Einblicke in das bestehende Portfolio und Ausblicke auf Zukünftiges werden Euch sicher inspirieren. Neben den großen Keynotes werden kleine interaktivere Formate  den 2. Tag zu einer Bereicherung machen.

Wenn Ihr Euch noch nicht registriert habt, solltet Ihr das schnell nachholen. Schnappt Euch noch schnell ein Early Bird Ticket. Nutzt die Chance, dabei zu sein, wenn wir mit Euch die aktuellsten Themen rund um künstliche Intelligenz und dem Clean Core Konzept diskutieren. Wir freuen uns auf Euch!

REGISTRIERUNG

Event Website

entry.title: Using Zebra Library in Build Apps
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm currently developing a mobile app using SAP Build Apps (formerly AppGuyver) for the Zebra TC72 device. The app's primary function is to scan product barcodes to retrieve information. However, I've encountered an issue with the Zebra TC72's optical barcode reader—it behaves like a keyboard and requires an active input field to function.

Ideally, I would like the scan input to directly populate the selected input field without the need for manual intervention. Each page of the app will contain only one input field or none at all.

I'm aware that there are libraries available that include Zebra functionalities in apps. However, I haven't been successful in integrating external tools into SAP Build Apps.

Does anyone have experience or insights on how to directly integrate barcode scanning functionality into SAP Build Apps?

Thank you for your assistance!

entry.title: KIT to stock process- batch determination of components at production order release
entry.content:
In the KIT to stock process, we have our batch determination of components during the release of production order, these batches are copied over to the outbound delivery and later gets distributed to EWM. 

In scenarios where the determined batch cannot be picked (could be missing material, incomplete qty, damages etc.), and a new batch needs to be picked during the OBD processing in EWM and be picked up by the WT. Is there a possibility with exception handling to handle such cases or what could be a possible solution in such cases ?

 

Thanks,

Sandeep

entry.title: Re: AI Foundation, SAP’s all-in-one AI toolkit for developers
entry.content:
Hi Paul,

It’s fascinating to see how SAP is leveraging AI to enhance business processes. The AI Foundation seems to be a comprehensive toolkit for developers, offering ready-to-use AI services that are customizable and grounded in business data. Thanks for the informative blog.

entry.title: MDG DQM: Creating complex validation rules for MDG classic mode in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi All,

In the series of DQM blogs, we have seen how to configure basic validation and derivation rules for MDG central governance and Mass processing. We have also seen how to troubleshoot DQM rules.

If you have missed these blogs here are the links to those articles.

MDG DQM: Employing rules in central governance & mass processing in S/4 HANA

MDG DQM: Empowering Your Business with High-Quality Data in S/4HANA

MDG DQM: Enriching product master data with derivation rules in S/4 HANA

MDG DQM: Troubleshooting DQM validation rules for central governance - Product Master S/4 HANA

INTRODUCTION:

DQM rules in MDG are an option to replace the traditional BADI, BRF+ based validation and derivation rules that need to be built specifically for each MDG application context. Also these rules doesn't allow us to run the Data quality evaluations to identify potential issues in the master data.

Some challenges & complexities?

Parallel workflows

Currently DQM validation rules for central governance have only specific attributes in the context like change request type, status, priority etc. Change request step which is mostly commonly used to write validations is not available in the context at this juncture as a result when we are running a parallel workflow we need to identify the step for which the rule should be run, so that parallel approvers are not affected by unnecessary errors.

Using additional tables not available in context of DQM structures

We often need to use additional tables both standard & custom, in such scenarios we might need to use additional BRF+ options like database look up , procedure calls etc while modelling the rules.

MAIN CONTENT

In this blog post, we will see how we can model complex rules in MDG DQM using BRF+ procedure calls. The scenario i have taken is a parallel workflow and how to trigger step specific validations.

1. We need to create a rule using Validation rule app. You can follow the steps from my blog post 

MDG DQM: Empowering Your Business with High-Quality Data in S/4HANA

2. In the condition expression in BRF+ we will have to model the rule differently for each rule stage like MDG central governance, Mass processing, consolidation, Since MDG central governance and mass processing have different underlying data models. We will create a case statement as below.

3. So for MDG central governance we create a boolean expression CHECK_MHDHB_MDF. Inside this boolean we will model the rule using a procedure call.

CHECK_CR_STEP_55 is the procedure call which will check if the CR step for the CR type is 55

 

4. In the procedure call, i am checking the CR step using the APP context class and set a flag as true when the step is 55 else it will be set as false.

CONCLUSION

With this blog post we saw how we can leverage additional BRF+ functions like procedure call to build complex rules. It also shows that DQM rules provides the flexibility to incorporate your own logic to build complex rules.
 
The Data Quality Management (DQM) rule marks a significant evolution from BADI-based rules within Master Data Governance (MDG) systems. With DQM, the realm of rule creation transcends previous limitations, where MDG users are completely dependent on IT team to tell them what rules are built in the system, what are enabled/disabled. This provides transparency into the system and help in shaping future of data governance with unparalleled flexibility and precision.
 
Regards
Anirban

entry.title: Custom field for Accounting Master Data - Internal Order
entry.content:
Dear Community

new Custom field for (Accounting) Master data on Internal Order is not becoming visible while creating a new master record (through Manage Internal Orders - Fiori App) after enabling and publishing the new field. Any steps additionally to Custom Fields Fiori app, like running an update program in with GUI transaction?

Kindly advise.

Best regards,
Frank

hello @SugguSandeep , thank you for replying.

I attach images of the procedure:

entry.content: Login on the user platform GE77570, I have already sent an email again to change the password, I have done everything and I cannot log in. Help

entry.title: Re: Page variable binded to boolean doesn't work
entry.content:
Hello Dan,

Sorry for the confused report, I'll send print to help illustrate what i want to say:

I have a variable called pageVars.booleanVar with the initial value = true

 My canvas has a photo, 2 containers (one with a spinner and the other with the credentials input) and a button:

I binded the variable to the spinner container visibility and the inverse value to the login container visibility so when show one don't show the other

 After I binded a flowfunction to invert the variable when tap on the button and a toast with "Done" after:

but when tapping the button nothing works, even setting the function to set the variable to false, still don't work:

And the toast doesn't pop up, but if i use an appVars it works.

I tried to share a screen record i did but i don't have permission to do that...

entry.title: Re: (how) can we use IN clause in $filter
entry.content: Hi David, is a list of the supported odata filter-criteria available anywhere in the cap-doc? It took us disproportionately long to find this blog-entry ensuring that this is a deficit of cap and not a user-error on our side. It would be nice not having to go through this again.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANa for Product Compliance Data Migration approach: Raw Materials - Bulk vs Packaged
entry.content:
Hi Paulo,

in Product Compliance New you should always create a compliance view for an unpackaged product (UP). In case you do have an actual unpackaged/bulk product master, you will link it. In case you don't have a product master, you will just create the compliance view object without any link to an unpackaged product master. In both cases you will link the packaged product masters in the unpackaged compliance view. So 2.1 and 2.2 could be addressed with one UP with links to the sold product masters. 2.3 would be separate UPs: one for the raw material and one for the sold product, which then uses the raw material in its BOM / material based composition.

Hope that helps.

Ralf

entry.title: Default document type in "Create Supplier Invoice" App
entry.content:
Dear all, 

is there an update on this topic? 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/how-to-change-default-accounting-document-type-in-quot-create-supplier/qaq-p/12307225

Is it meanwhile possible to change the default document type for supplier invoices or credit memos entered via "create supplier invoice"-App?

Thanks, and best regards, 

Linda

entry.title: Re: Receiver XI adapter in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi @manigram,

It must be the request for OAuth2 authentication at the CPI end-point - which is required to retrieve a Bearer Token used to send the payload to Integration Suite.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Unlocking Proactive Solutions: Preventative Content now Live in SAP for Me
entry.content:
What you already know:

  • What priority solutions are support currently working on that could prevent business impacting issues on my systems (hot solutions).
  • What solutions, relevant for my systems are other customers viewing most today (trending solutions).  

What is inew?

  • What existing solutions (provided to other customers) are relevant for my systems (preventative solutions – initially available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud only).

You will get your proactive solutions through the new ‘Preventative KBA & Notes’ card within the “Services & Support” dashboard in SAP for Me. The card now gives you easy access to preventative, hot and trending solutions for the systems you are interested in. Preventative knowledge based articles (KBAs) are solutions (based on similarities) that are identified by artificial intelligence (AI) services to notify you of potential issues with your software. The card also integrates the existing real-time hot & trending solution recommendations which together with preventative solutions helps you minimize disruptions, react immediately, and anticipate and mitigate potential risks for your software solution landscape.

Where to find it?

To access the proactive solutions, go to ‘Services & Support’ dashboard > ‘KBAs & Notes’ tab > ‘Preventative KBAs & Notes’ card

  • Upon your very first visit, KBAs & notes for your favourite systems are automatically listed, provided you have marked ten or fewer systems as favourites. If you do not have favourites, then you can simply use the filter to select the systems you are interested in.
  • For your next visit, the card shows preventative, hot, and trending solutions for the systems that you had previously selected.

Refining your solutions

  • Click the respective column header to sort the list by number, title, type, category, or component that listed solutions are assigned to, or by system affected by the solution, or by alert date (the point in time when the note was identified as preventative, hot or trending).
  • Use the filters Component and Type, or use Search to narrow the list of notes down to the ones you are most interested in.
  • Choose the 3-dot menu, then Export, to download the table to a local file and share with relevant colleagues.
  • The green banner shows your current system selection and with one click will bring you to the system selection filter.

What to do next?

  • Continue to enhance the AI services behind the card with new data models to achieve greater relevancy & accuracy of preventative solutions.
  • A key task on our roadmap is to expand the AI services to cover multiple products (Please note, as of today, the preventative solutions are only available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud)
  • Add further filtering capabilities to help you further refine your solutions.

entry.title: Re: Change the idtype from 04 to 00 or 01 without the address number
entry.content: It seems you've entered this question at the wrong SAP Managed Tag. SAP HANA is not S/4HANA. HANA is the database platform whereas S/4 is SAP's new ERP system.

entry.title: Re: SAP Background Job Processing
entry.content: I believe you have posted a question here instead of a blog....

entry.title: Re: Load standard fiori always
entry.content: What do you mean by load standard Fiori? Could you please detail a little bit more your question?

entry.title: Re: /home/user/projects/MyHANAApp is not a deployable CAP project.
entry.content: I face the same problem, this is completely odd since it worked just fine 2 days ago when I last deployed.

entry.title: Re: want to sync Users and groups from ADFS to customer IAS
entry.content:
Hi @jihoonkim01,

Please clarify what you mean/understand about sync user from ADFS to IAS. Is the customer expecting to migrate all users to IPS or are they simply looking to have an integration between IAS and ADFS?

If the intent is simply integration, then please review the following links:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/btpcfsec/3361873369.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-configure-ms-adfs-3-0-as-identity-provider-for-sap-hana-cloud/ba-p/13331791

If it is user migration and replacement of ADFS with IPS, the answer might be more complex. Please let me know.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Import message mapping from SAP PO to Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are trying to import message mapping from SAP PO to Cloud Integration.

But, I get this error when trying to do so, could you help to know.

Can't we import the message mapping with parameterized mapping in to Cloud Integration?

I see there are parameters are being used in SAP, is that the cause of this issue while trying to migrate this mapping object from SAP PO to SAP Integration Suite(Cloud Integration)?

Thanks

Ramu

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP SDK for iOS 10.0 is now available

by example the new FUIKeyValueTableViewCell has not been tested in Dark mode and the font of the title is not consistent compare to other form kit cells, I already submitted 2 incidents regarding that

also another bug in the Calendar about selection date range that was broken in 10.0.1, I have been told it will be fixed in 10.0.2

Hi guys,

We have created few guided tours but the Guided Tours tab in the carousel is not visible to the end users (please refer below screenshot) but authors can see it. We ensured that we have published the created guided tours.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: BTP Destination for external service with permanent Bearer Token/API key
entry.content: Did this work?

entry.title: Re: APC (TCP/Websockets) connection for SAP BTP ABAP envi...
entry.content:
Hi @stem21,

As a rule of thumb all apps and SaaS solutions running on BTP will be focusing on the HTTP protocol for all communication with the external world.

I highly doubt that ABAP Runtime will feature websockets communication as it is rated as 'tier 3' under the 'Clean Core' strategy. Therefore, it won't be something you will be able to achieve via the 'ABAP Cloud language set'.

You could implement via Cloud Foundry like so:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-use-websockets-in-the-cloud-foundry-environment-in-the-sap-cloud/ba-p/13365007

Or you could use Cloud Connector with the websocket channel:

https://me.sap.com/notes/2573250

Eventually you could have our message integration broker (Cloud Integration Suite) consuming messages from a queue via Websockets. 

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANa for Product Compliance Data Migration approach: Raw Materials - Bulk vs Packaged
entry.content:
Thank you RalfC

Your insight is most helpful.

I'll perform some tests with your advice in regard and will report back here how it worked.

Paulo Sérgio Pinto

entry.title: Unable to agreggate total member leaves of parent in advance formula Data Action script
entry.content:
Hi, I am trying to script a data action advance formula to calculate the sum OPEX by 3 categories and calculate VAT on each category.

I'm having trouble using the GLAccount for each category as it says its not a leaf member.

How do cant seem to figure out the syntax for sum all the leaf members of the each OPEX category parent. The leaf members at to many to list out and sometimes can vary.

CONFIG.HIERARCHY = [d/GLACCOUNT].[h/Hierarchcy]
CONFIG.TIME_HIERARCHY = FISCALYEAR

MEMBERSET [d/Date] = [d/Version].[p/START_MONTH] TO [d/Version].[p/END_MONTH] 
MEMBERSET [d/AUDITID] = "INPUT"
MEMBERSET [d/COMPANYCODE] = "HPCL"
MEMBERSET [d/GLACCOUNT] = BASEMEMBER ([d/GLACCOUNT], "DB100Parent","DB200Parent","DB300Parent")

VARIABLEMEMBER #OPEX_VAT OF [d/GLACCOUNT]
//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
//Calculation and Data Writing
//-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DATA([d/GLACCOUNT]= "VAT_OPEX_UTIL", [d/SEGMENT] = "#", [d/PROFITCENTER] = "#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/GLACCOUNT].[p/Hierarchy="DB300Parent")

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANa for Product Compliance Data Migration app...
entry.content:
Thank You Christoph for taking your time to answer my question.

The fact, though, is that I am talking about (SAP S/4HANA for) Product Compliance "new", and not (SAP S/4HANA for) EHS, or any of the classic solutions, as the customer is already on S/4HANA (2023), and not ECC.

As such I guess the scenarios I've posted, may be a bit less confusing when it comes to PC, but I admit they need some clarification; I'll address that as soon as I'm able.

Nevertheless, some your insights are already helpful in clearing some my doubts.

entry.title: Re: BTP ABAP system start\stop
entry.content:
Hi @KirillPalkov,

If this is a trial BTP account, then instances that consume Compute Units or that are based on Uptime metrics will be stopped every end-of-the-day. This cannot be overcome by any means. It is by design done this way as means to save resources on trial landscapes. 

If this is happening with you on a productive landscape, then you should open a support ticket for further investigation, as the system should never have an auto-stop scheduled on your account. 

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Qualtrics & Sf LMS Integration Best Practice & Experi...
entry.content: Hi @BrandyPelzel, thank you very much for sharing. I have used the Level 1 & 3 survey that comes with SF LMS organically. However, there are some functional areas that we find not fitting for our needs and we are hoping to implement L1 & 3 through Qualtrics instead.

entry.title: Cloud Integration: Advanced Event Mesh Adapter, Client Certificate, Solace PubSub+
entry.content:
SAP Cloud Integration (aka CPI) offers an “Advanced Event Mesh Adapter” which is well integrated with the “Advanced Event Mesh” broker.
This article shows how to set up a scenario where we send a message from iFlow via “Advanced Event Mesh Adapter” to Solace PubSub+ with Client Certificate Authentication.
Please refer to the sibling article series which describes in detail the setup for "AMQP Adapter".
The creation of a certificate chain is equal.
The configuration in Solace is also same.
So basically, in this blog post we only need to show the configuration in the “Advanced Event Mesh” (AEM) adapter.

Overview

  1. Introduction
  2. Create Client Certificate
  3. Configure Solace
  4. Create iFlow
  5. Run Scenario
  6. Key Takeaways

Prerequisites

🔹To follow this tutorial, access to a Solace Event Broker is required.
    This tutorial is based on a developer account at Solace PubSub+ Cloud 
🔹Access To CPI Tenant is required
🔹I recommend bookmarking the Security Glossary

1. Introduction

We want to send a message from an iFlow to a (standalone) Solace Broker.
We want to use the “Advanced Event Mesh” adapter.
Authentication should be configured with client certificate.

There’s a sibling blog post which uses the AMQP adapter for the same use case.
Almost all steps are equal and described in detail there.

What is the scenario?
This is the high-level overview of the simple scenario:

 We can see that the iFlow sends messages via "Advanced Event Mesh adapter" to a queue in Solace Event Broker.
On both sides, certificates have to be configured.

How to get certificates?
The sibling article series explains in detail multiple ways of generating certificates for this purpose.

To give an overview of some possibilities:

Use CPI Keystore dashboard

🔷 Create key pair / certificate in CPI
     🔹Use it as self-signed root in our scenario
     🔹Download the corresponding CSR (certificate signing request) and create a self-signed chain on laptop
     🔹Download the CSR and use it to purchase a productive chain from productive CA
🔷 Download existing CPI client certificate chain and use it for our scenario

Use Laptop

🔷 Create key pair / certificate ourselves on laptop
     🔹Use Openssl
          - Create self-signed root or chain (chain with different length)
          - Create CSR and purchase productive chain
     🔹Use Java Keytool
          - etc
     🔹Use any other tool
          - etc

Use BTP

🔷let service broker or IAs generate key pair / certificate chain and use that for our scenario

In the present tutorial, we’re going the easy way and use a CPI-generated certificate.
I recommend however going through the experience of creating certificate chain on local computer.

2. Create Client Certificate

Using the built-in functionality of CPI to generate a key pair has the following advantage:
The private key is generated at CPI, where it is needed.
The private key never leaves CPI (it is not possible to download it)
The private key does not need to be uploaded to CPI via net.
The disadvantage would be that this private key cannot be used locally, as it is not possible to  download it.

For today’s article, we’re using the CPI keystore to create a self-signed certificate.
This will serve as trusted root certificate at solace-side, and it will be used as client-certificate in iFlow.

Ceate Key Pair in CPI

We go to keystore and choose "Create -> Key Pair"
We enter some data of your choice, e.g.
     🔸Alias:  “demokeypair”
     🔸CN: “demokeypair”

It will be used for creating the certificate.

 After creation, we click on the new entry I the dashboard to view the details:

 

We can see that a self-signed certificate has been created.
If subject and issuer DN are equal, then this means it is a root certificate.

Now we can download the new certificate via context button and “Download Certificate”
In my example, a file demokeypair.cer is downloaded to my laptop.

Note:
If you encounter a .p7b file after download artifacts, you can extract it with the help of OpenSSL and the pkcs7 command.

 

 

 

openssl pkcs7 -in demokeypair.p7b -print_certs -out certificate.cer

 

 

 

3. Configure Solace Event Broker

Now that we have the client certificate, we can establish trust at Solace side.
This means:
Solace  receives an authentication request with a client certificate.
It must validate the certificate.
It must trust it.

To do so, the signature of the issuer is checked – and the issuer itself – continuing the chain up to the root.
If the root is not known by Solace, then it has to be configured.
As such, we have to upload our certificate (which is a root as well) to Solace.

See sibling blog post for detailed description.

3.1. Upload Root Certificate

We go to “Cluster Manager” ➡️ Event Broker service instance ➡️  “Manage” tab.
➡️click “Certificate Authorities” and upload the root certificate from previous chapter.
In my example, we upload the demokeypair.cer file.

➡️ Press “Create”.
➡️ Press “Enable”.
➡️ Press “Apply”.

3.2. Authorization for Client

We go to “Cluster Manager” ➡️ Event Broker service instance ➡️  “PubSub+ Broker Manager”.
➡️  “Access Control” ➡️ “Client Usernames”.
Create new client username and enter the value of the “CN” of our client certificate from previous chapter.
In my example: “demokeypair”

3.3. Create Queue

We go to “Open PubSub+ Broker Manager” and choose “Queues” on the left navigation pane.
Then we create a new queue  and enter a name of our choice, e.g. “demo”

3.4. Connection Details

To find the connection details that we need in the next chapter, we go to
 “Cluster Manager” ➡️ Event Broker service instance ➡️ “Connect” tab.
Expand “Solace Messaging” section (SMF over TCP).
Copy the “Message VPN” and “SMF Host”:

4. Create iFlow

We create a very simple iFlow that does nothing than sending an empty message to the Solace broker.
See sibling blog post for some more description.

Other than in sibling post, we use the “Advanced Event Mesh” adapter.

Configure Advanced Event Mesh adapter
🔸URL
We need to enter the full URL of the SMF host, which we copied in previous chapter.
Including protocol and port.
Just copy&paste from Solace dashboard (previous chapter).
🔸Message VPN
The Message VPN can also be copied from Solace.
🔸Username
The Username “default” can be used, as it is available on Solace as per default.
But any other existing username can be entered here as well.
🔸Keystore Alias
The Keystore Alias must point to a key pair entry.
It is checked during deployment.
In our example, we enter the key pair which we created in chapter above: “demokeypair”

In the “Processing” Tab, we choose “Queue” and enter the name of the queue we created above:

That’s it, we can save the iFlow.

5. Run Scenario

To run the scenario we just deploy the iFlow.
Then we check the result:
In CPI : The log at “Monitor Message Processing” should show success message.
In event broker: The number of “Messages Queued” should have increased.

6. Quick Guide

🔷Certificate Chain:
    🔹The intermediate certificate must have CA:TRUE
    🔹The chain order: Root Rear.
🔷Solace:
    🔹Upload the root cert.
    🔹Create username equal to “CN”.
    🔹Username must be set to "Enabled".
🔷CPI:
    🔹Upload the chain, not only client cert.
        Reason: the whole chain must be sent to Solace, as at Solace only the root is known.

Summary

In this blog post we’ve learned how to deal with client certificates.
We wanted to use certificate-based authentication instead of username / password (SASL).
To do so, we learned how to easily get a key pair and a certificate.
To establish trust, we need to  configure the target server (Solace) with the trusted root certificate.
Finally, we can use the certificate in CPI, which means, it is stored in the Keystore and used in the iflow.
In this blog post, we’ve learned how to configure the Advanced Event Mesh adapter.
There’s a sibling article series which explains many details about certificates.

🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉🎉

entry.title: Re: Not able to access Appgyver composer / Classic sap ap...
entry.content: Hi, to everyone replying here to me - note that I don't receive notifications unless you tag me directly or send a PM. I have now reached out to everyone concerning their community projects, and the old version is now closed.

entry.title: Open ODS Views association with a condition
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

i have multiple fields in a composite provider that i want to associate with an open ODS View which has their corresponding texts. The ODS View has a field called "Level", that can go from 0 to 5. 
What i want is to have an association that does the following:
- Field 1: has text where "level" = 0
- Field 2: has text where "level" = 1
and so on...

How can i associate my fields with the ODS View while using this "Level" Condition?

Thank you for your time

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: Week 4
entry.content:
My submission:

Publish:

Consume:

Hash: 752b8fb53bdab4a8575f1c197c366274085f1fadf1e1e47077fe9aafd7c41b09

entry.title: Re: Entering debugging with ADT ends up always in SAPMSSYD
entry.content:
Hi @jrgkraus, @Sandra_Rossi,

ADT communicates with the SAP Backend server via HTTP - which is not the same as what happens when you call it via '/h'. Therefore you won't be able to use this 'technique' to debug any more. Furthermore, it doesn't really make any sense to debug transactions on ADT as its primarily used to develop under a new paradigm (using RAP and "ABAP Cloud Language Set".

This is by design on ADT and it won't work also under the GUI for HTML.

If you really need this as you don't really care about 'clean core', then you must review the following note:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3119720/E

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: APC (TCP/Websockets) connection for SAP BTP ABAP envi...
entry.content:
Hi Ivan,

 

thank you for your reply. I agree is it not a modern technology like http. But I do not understand why it should inflict Clean Core Stragety: every handling in the system related to the message could be done via RAP objects. TCP is just a matter of communication.

Regarding cloud foundry: this was also a reason why I am asking. SAP is providing TCP communication via Cloud Foundry but not for the "native" ABAP environment. Somehow a disadvantage when using ABAP on BTP. 

Will check on the Websocket for Cloud Connector though - thanks!

PS: my question is directed also in TCP direction (not only websockets).

Best regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Joining us from the AppGyver Forum? Start here in the SAP Builders Group!
entry.content:
Hi @Fadhilah I have sent you a private message with further instructions.

To everyone else reading, as of now - we have closed access to the old community version. It is a manual effort to keep it running. Please continue your work with the new community tool.

@Josephrezende I have responded to your feedback on your latest post in the group, but please as a reminder, let's keep the discussions here as civil as possible.

entry.title: Re: Mass data processing to compare addresses
entry.content: I already found now a solution by making packages per city.

entry.title: Re: Joining us from the AppGyver Forum? Start here in the SAP Builders Group!
entry.content: Hi @Mihu_  - we are working on a solution to make the old forum content available here in the new groups. While we prepare this, can you let me know if there's a particular topic you're searching for?

entry.title: Re: Process Automation: Capital Expenditure Approval Proc...
entry.content:
Hi @a_oezsoy,

Open the package.json and review the nodeJS version there. Apparently, there is no support for version 16. So all you need to do is switch to version 18 instead.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: SAP BFC - "Sub-parent reporting Unit"
entry.content:
Hi All,

Can anyone help?

Entity A consolidates Entity B via equity method at 50%. In turn, Entity B fully consolidates Entities C, D and E at 100%. Is there a way to reflect this structure in "scope builder" in SAP BFC?

I have tried to create a hierarchical scope and then select Entity B as a "sub- parent reporting unit (SP) ". However this does not seem to be possible as Entity B is in equity method? Does anyone know if there is a way to solve this? 

Thanks in advance!

AleP

entry.title: Re: Joining us from the AppGyver Forum? Start here in the SAP Builders Group!
entry.content:
Hello @EsmeeX,

I replied back a few hours.

Again, sorry for the angry replies, it won't happen again!

entry.title: Re: Mass data processing to compare addresses
entry.content: Nice idea (country/city)

entry.title: Re: BTP's Odata consumed by ECC
entry.content:
Hi @msasinski, 

What do you mean by "OData API hold" ?

Once you clarify what you want to do in terms of which API, then I might be able to provide you with documentation related to it.

As a rule of thumb, each service instance on BTP will have a "Service Key" associated or manually created. Such "service key" will contain all credentials required to access the service. Usually they will support the OAuth2 Client Credentials flow. Therefore, on that object you will find the service end-point, the token end-point, the client ID and client Secret that are required for authentication to the API.

There is the API Business Accelerator Hub - which is an API catalog for all SAP products - where you can find detailed information for each API:

https://api.sap.com/

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: SAP AI Core not exist on trial account
entry.content: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-free-tier-account.html

entry.title: Re: BTP's Odata consumed by ECC
entry.content:
thank you for the answer. I mean "hold" as contain, host etc. I am looking to execute this API:

https://api.sap.com/api/SAP_ICSM_StudyService/path/get_Studies__ID__

You inspired me for some different google search and found something that looks up to date

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/simple-and-flexible-oauth-2-0-rest-api-call-from-an-abap-program-s-4hana-on/ba-p/13573754

I will keep asking our BTP team for the API endpoint

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Ask Me Anything Series: SAP Build Code
entry.content: Thanks for you comment @Devraj ! We hope you enjoyed the session. For more information on SAP Build Code, please check out the community page: SAP Build Code | SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Hazardous substance register list (Gefahrstoffkataste...
entry.content:
Hello again.

Maybe PC's Substance Volume Tracking function can be used to develop a Fiori App to deal with your need? 
I'm just theorizing at the moment, mind you, and I know it might be a way too long stretch.

entry.title: Re: How to create single Transfer Order+Item for same material PO lines after GR is posted in Wareho
entry.content: Hi Deepak , Do you have any solution on this requirement? I do have same requirement and searching for a solution.Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Generate Time Forms from Time Admin Workbench
entry.content: Is it possible to send a form with bulkaction?  I want to send a mail for alot employee at once a time

entry.title: Re: Why SAP killed Appgyver and made everything worse?
entry.content:
Hi Joseph. So just to clarify this statement:

"...Please note that while we welcome any feedback about the community experience, this forum in particular is the Builders Group, so the topics here should be focused on SAP Build products specifically..."

Are you referring to the main subject of my post? Isn't SAP Build Apps the Community Edition? 

I apologize if my communication was unclear around this. SAP Build Apps is the name of our enterprise product (formerly SAP AppGyver). We also offer the Community Edition (formerly known as AppGyver Community Edition), which is the free version of this product that many developers have used for years. We will have more news regarding the official name of this soon, which will hopefully make it clearer when we write blog posts, etc.

This Builders Group is open for users of the entire SAP Build low-code portfolio, which includes SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Process Automation. More official information on SAP Build here: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/low-code.html

So anyone that's interested in SAP Build products (and that includes the community edition of SAP Build Apps) should engage and connect here in the Builders Group.

You're completely right that the SAP ecosystem has many products and topics, and it's overwhelming to the newcomers. I am also relatively new to SAP and I find it to be a lot! To be clear, my blog post was solely focused on letting the AppGyver users know how they can quickly onboard to this new Builders Group and use it to connect with us and report their learnings/issues/etc. And also, the SAP Community has many improvements needed to make it easier to navigate between all the topics and separate product pages. Again, we're happy to pass along feedback about the SAP Community experience!

I'll look again at the blog post, but if you have any specific suggestions for something I could add to the article to make it clearer, I appreciate the input.

entry.title: Coexisting versions of SAM / SSAM (2010 & 2310)
entry.content:
Hi community,

I have an Utilities client who wants to upgrade to SSAM 2310 gradually, one business process at a time, due to heavy customizations per proccess in the app.
Meanwhile, needs to keep using in test and production the other processes in the current SAM 2010 version.
If we apply the needed TCI notes for the upgrade and activate the 2310 BC Set...

Would there be risks or backwards compatibility issues?

Thanks in advance for your help.
Gonzalo.

entry.title: Re: How to connect sap b1 to excel so that I can get live...
entry.content:
Dear Samrat,
you should have at least two options:  
1) For HANA you can try Interactive Analyses. 
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_ONE/68a2e87fb29941b5bf959a184d9c6727/b21f38ad5c82426296d2c9f99b792781.html

2) Or you use direct queries to the hana database. For that you would need a HANA DB User with "select" permission to execute the query. 
In Excel you can also select HANA as a database. 

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: Load standard fiori always
entry.content:
Hi Ivan

you can create variants, one as Standard and i created one custom variant. even if i create custom variant when i click on fiori it should load Standard app always.

entry.title: How to assign Batch for GR materials( 101 mvt type) while using BAPI_PRODORDCONF_CREATE_TT
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

         At CO11N, system is providing Automatically Batch for Material with Mvt type 101.

       Now a third party system is sending Production Order (AUFNR) and YIELD. 

    I'm using BAPI_PRODORDCONF_CREATE_TT for Production confirmation. System is doing 261 mvt type(GI) but not GR (101 mvt type) . I can see the error in COGI as "ENTER BATCH"

Need guidance in this regard. 

 

Thanks & regards

Sandeep

entry.title: Re: How to reverse operation status from JBFI REL to REL...

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

entry.title: SAP MDG – A Stepping Stone for SAP S/4HANA Journey – Part 3
entry.content:
Introduction

Clients across the globe are getting into the Transformation journey started with SAP S/4HANA.

In this blog, we will take one of the use cases to discuss how the master data (MDG)  journey will be the stepping stone for SAP S/4HANA Transformation and the needed steps to achieve it.

This is the third blog on this topic. For Part 1 & 2 , kindly refer

https://blogs.sap.com/2023/01/27/sap-mdg-a-stepping-stone-for-sap-s-4hana-journey-part-i/

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-mdg-a-stepping-stone-for-sap-s-4hana-journey-part-2/ba-p/13579098

Key Aspects for MDG : Deep Dive

B. MDG Specific

B.1 Data Model

As MDG going to the central master data systems catering for 3 ECC systems & future S/4 system, it is important to finalize the fields ( data model) to be governed & strategy for maintenance

Approach for Fields Finalization

3 ECC systems are using the same master data object ( Ex. Customer) with various fields depending upon the business process.

Following is the high-level overview through which the fields needed for MDG governance can be obtained

  • As-Is Analysis: Field usages by Account Groups & ECC systems

The current usages of field across multiple account groups & ECC systems needs to be analysed

There are multiple tools which helps to get this data cardinality report from the given data sources.  Such reports need to be brought up for this analysis

There would be multiple scenarios in this case based on the report, a sample given below

For easy understanding, I have given as High, Medium and Low. In the real world, it needs to be defined as the % of data availability in that field.

  • As-Is Analysis: Master Data Form

The next step would be to understand the fields used from the current business process perspective.

Master data forms used in the current process will help to understand the fields collected from businesses.

Also if there are any fields derived in the form/system as per business rules, then those fields also need to be understood and considered.

  • To-Be Finalization

Based on the insights from the system & master data form , the To-Be fields need to be finalized as follows

There could be multiple combinations on this matrix. It needs business discussions & agreement for the fields if it needs to be excluded from To-Be scope

Once the list of fields are finalized, then it needs to be checked in MDG whether it is already available in Out of the Box data model. If its not the case, the below topic – Strategy for MDG governance should be considered

  • Field Status (M/O/R/H)

Once the fields for the To-Be data model is finalized, the next step would be the field status finalization by each account groups

The below picture shows the current field status for each field by account group & systems ( As-Is state) which shows differences

M – Mandatory ; O – Optional ; R – Read-Only ; H – Hidden

The following would be the approach to finalizing the future Field status

If there are differences, then there needs a thinking on the future and take up the next steps.

Knowing the vision that SAP MDG would be the foundation of future SAP S/4HANA, however, it also needs to support the legacy world. So the solution design should be a bridge between the legacy & future world.

Also, not all business processes have been optimized & standardized as part of this program. So, there will be differences in terms of field being used and their field status.

Due to this, it calls for the flexibility of MDG solution in terms of the field status. One of the proposal would be

  • Wherever the field status is the same (Either M/O/R/H) across all ECC systems, in the To-Be (MDG) it will be the same (Either M/O/R/H) and it will be configured at the back end
  • However if the field status is different, then in the To-Be (MDG) , do the Optional be considered?
    • Say O- Optional is majority, then it can be configured at the back end
    • Also keeping Optional at the back end configuration will help to overcome the forced need of data enrichment at local systems for data migration in to MDG & also for CRUD process

The need for a specific ECC system field status ( Ex: Field 2 for ECC – Americas to be mandatory for Acc grp 1) to be catered via UI and/or validation. Solution approach will be described in their relevant sections

Strategy for MDG Governance

SAP provides out of the box data model for the master data objects ( Customer, Vendor, Material & Finance). Not all standard SAP fields are part of OOB data model.

However, SAP MDG supports the extensibility of the data model. It needs to be considered as development (WRICEF) in that case.

A strategic discussion & decision to be made in terms of the fields governance in MDG. There are a couple of options for this as described below

In the next blog, I will cover other topics in detail.

About the Author,

Antony Prasanna has been working in SAP master data space for 18+ years. Have experience working across multiple large transformation programs on SAP S/4HANA including SAP MDM, MDG and Data Migration tools.

entry.title: Epm connection established but when i click to open reports it is not showing showing server report
entry.content:
after connecting to EPM from SAP BPC 10.1 i was able to login but when i click to open tab it is showing half loaded image.

 

 can anyone help me with this what to do here as i have reinstalled EPM several time that dosn't worked.

entry.title: Re: Qualtrics & Sf LMS Integration Best Practice & Experi...
entry.content: Hi Bumin, I'm curious about the functionality you are looking for with the surveys? Is it for more dashboard capabilities or some other reason? Also, is it possible to use L3 & L3 surveys in Qualtrics automatically? The impression I had is that the integration was for more generic surveys that would be on the Learning page itself asking about overall course opportunities, system navigation, etc.
